Output ONLY one fenced ```json code block for Simple Host, and nothing else. Use this exact schema:
{"simpleHost":1,"name":"[YOUR-SITE-NAME]","files":{"index.html":"<the full, self-contained page as escaped text, with inline CSS and JS>"}}
Relative links only — never a leading slash. index.html must be one self-contained file (inline CSS/JS).

SITE: [Describe your site in plain language here — what it's for, the style/colors/vibe, the font, any headings, and all the real details like names, dates, times, and locations. Say what visitors should be able to do.]

BACKEND — FOLLOW EXACTLY, DO NOT IMPROVISE.
This site is hosted at https://sites.simple-host.app/<handle>/<name>/ and has a real, free JSON backend that you MUST use.
HARD BAN: never use localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, or any client-only/in-memory storage for shared data (sign-ups, RSVPs, counts, messages). Shared data MUST live on the server via fetch(). Do NOT invent endpoints. Do NOT hardcode the handle or the site name.

Put this EXACT route setup at the very top of your <script>:
const m = location.pathname.match(/^\/([a-z0-9-]+)\/([a-z0-9-]+)/);
const BASE = m ? '/v1/u/' + m[1] + '/sites/' + m[2] : null;
const STATE = BASE ? BASE + '/state' : null;
const LIST  = BASE ? BASE + '/collections/entries' : null;

STEP 1 — Save an entry (do BOTH awaits):
await fetch(LIST, {method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({ /* the entry's fields, e.g. name, guests: Number(guests) */ })});
await fetch(STATE, {method:'PATCH', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({ops:[{op:'inc', path:'total', by: 1}]})});
(If you're counting people rather than entries, use by: Number(guests) instead of by: 1.)

STEP 2 — Live count:
async function loadCount(){ if(!STATE) return; const s = await fetch(STATE).then(r=>r.json()).catch(()=>({})); document.getElementById('count').textContent = (s && s.total) || 0; }
Call loadCount() on page load, again right after a successful save, and on a setInterval every 4000ms. Make sure there is an element with id="count" on the page.

STEP 3 — After a successful save, hide the form and show a friendly confirmation message, then clear the inputs.

STEP 4 — Visitor messages/comments: do NOT hand-build these. Use Simple Host's built-in comments widget by adding EXACTLY these three lines immediately before </body>, and nothing else for messages:
- a <script> tag whose content is:  window.SH_COMMENTS={accent:"#HEXCOLOR", title:"YOUR TITLE"}
- then:  <section id="sh-comments"></section>
- then a <script> tag with src="https://simple-host.app/comments.js" and the defer attribute
(Only include Step 4 if your site actually needs a comments/message section.)

STEP 5 — Use a SOLID background color, not a gradient (the comments widget reads light/dark from a solid background; a gradient breaks it). The backend store is PUBLIC — never put secrets in it.

STEP 6 — A PLACE TO VIEW WHAT'S COLLECTED (do this WHENEVER the site saves entries — RSVPs, sign-ups, messages, orders). A form with nowhere to read the results is useless, so ALWAYS add a SECOND file "admin.html" to the files{} map (files can hold more than one file):
- admin.html derives BASE/LIST/STATE from location.pathname the SAME way, then does GET LIST + '?limit=200' and renders EVERY entry in a clean table (all fields, newest first) plus the live total from STATE. Load on open and refresh every 5000ms. Response shape: { items:[ { id, data:{...your fields}, created_at } ], next }.
- Match the site's visual style. Put <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> in its <head>. Do NOT build a fake password gate (the data is public by URL — say so in one small line rather than pretending it's locked).
- From index.html, link to it quietly: a small "Organizer view →" link (href="admin.html") in the footer.

SELF-CHECK before you answer: (a) zero uses of localStorage/sessionStorage/cookies anywhere; (b) fetch() is actually called three ways — POST to LIST, PATCH to STATE, and GET to STATE; (c) if used, the three comments lines are present verbatim; (d) BASE/STATE/LIST are derived from location.pathname and NOT hardcoded; (e) background is a solid color; (f) if the site collects anything, files{} ALSO contains admin.html that GETs LIST and renders the entries, linked from index.html. If any check fails, fix it before outputting. Output only the JSON block.
